title: convolvulus bona-nox
intro: Convolvulus bona-nox, also known as bindweed or creeping jenny, is a perennial plant in the morning glory family native to Europe and western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of alkaloids like scopoletin. The health benefits of Convolvulus bona-nox include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
